description: For the Public Service Internship Programme and other government roles. Emphasises public service ethos, discipline, and administrative competence.

examples.title: Public Service Internship Programme Application
examples.body:
1 March 2026

Dear Director,

I am writing to apply for the Public Service Internship Programme. I am a recent graduate of the University of Zambia with a Bachelor of Arts in Public Administration, and I am eager to serve the Zambian people through the public service.

My decision to pursue a career in public service was shaped by my upbringing in a rural community where I witnessed both the challenges of service delivery and the dedication of public servants who worked under difficult conditions to improve lives. I believe that effective public administration is the foundation of national development, and I want to be part of building that foundation.

My academic training has provided me with knowledge in public policy, organisational management, public finance, and administrative law. I have completed coursework in strategic planning, performance management, and e-government, all of which are directly relevant to modernising public service delivery. I am also proficient in Microsoft Office and have basic knowledge of government accounting through a short course I completed online.

I am a disciplined, punctual, and respectful person who understands the importance of hierarchy, protocol, and confidentiality in government work. I am prepared to work in any department assigned to me, whether it is policy, administration, finance, or operations, and to learn from experienced civil servants.

I am deeply committed to the values of integrity, professionalism, and service to the nation. I would be honoured to contribute to the Public Service Internship Programme and to begin my career as a servant of the Zambian people.

Yours faithfully,

Edward Chola

examples.title: Government Attachment at Ministry of Finance
examples.body:
15 May 2026

Dear Permanent Secretary,

I am applying for an attachment position in the Budget and Economic Affairs Department at the Ministry of Finance. I am a fourth-year student at the University of Zambia studying Economics, with a focus on public finance and development economics.

My academic training has given me a strong understanding of macroeconomic policy, fiscal management, and public expenditure analysis. I have completed coursework in national income accounting, monetary economics, development planning, and econometrics. For my research project, I analysed the effectiveness of the Farmer Input Support Programme using econometric methods, finding that while the programme increased maize yields, its cost-effectiveness could be improved through better targeting.

I am particularly interested in budget formulation and expenditure tracking. I believe that transparent and efficient public spending is essential for development, and I want to learn how the budget process works from the inside. I am also interested in debt sustainability analysis, given Zambia's recent economic challenges, and I would welcome the opportunity to contribute research or analysis in this area.

I am a meticulous, analytically minded student who takes pride in accuracy and attention to detail. I understand that government work involves long hours, careful documentation, and adherence to procedures. I am prepared for all of that.

I am available for a six-month attachment from June to November 2026.

Yours faithfully,

Nathan Mwansa

examples.title: Local Government Administration Internship
examples.body:
20 June 2026

Dear Town Clerk,

I am writing to apply for the internship position in your administration department. I am a third-year student at the University of Zambia studying Local Government Administration, and I am eager to gain practical experience in municipal governance.

My programme has covered local government law, urban planning, revenue collection, service delivery management, and community participation. I have studied the Local Government Act, the decentralisation policy framework, and the challenges facing local authorities in Zambia, including limited resources, institutional capacity gaps, and community engagement difficulties.

During a field visit to a local council in my second year, I observed council meetings, reviewed committee reports, and interviewed ward councillors about their roles and challenges. The experience gave me practical insight into the gap between policy and implementation at the local level, and it strengthened my commitment to working in local government.

I am a community-minded person who believes that local government is the level of government closest to the people, and therefore the most important. I am patient, respectful, and skilled at listening, qualities that are essential when dealing with community members who may be frustrated with service delivery.

I am available for a twelve-month internship starting in July 2026. I would be grateful for the opportunity to serve your council and to learn from your experienced administrators.

Yours faithfully,

Sarah Mutinta
